races.race_name: Racing TV Amateur Jockeys' Handicap

runners.name: Shimmering Sands
runners.spotlight: Stuck his neck in front in the final strides over C&D (good) on seasonal debut, ending a three-year losing spell; still well handicapped on peak form and commands respect under very promising young amateur Henry Callan, who has built up an impressive strike-rate.
runners.comment: C&D winner on seasonal debut; respected under very promising amateur Henry Callan

runners.name: Star Start
runners.spotlight: Had wind surgery (his third) since last run; form at Pontefract features a success last August (off 2lb higher; one of three wins under Charli Parker) and best effort this season; record of 0-19 when racing above Class 6 is the negative.
runners.comment: Record of 0-19 when racing above Class 6 tempers enthusiasm

runners.name: Ephron
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 3yo whose form since handicapping features a reappearance win at Redcar (1m) in April and keeping-on second at Carlisle (1m1f) last time; may still have further progress in him and this return to 1m2f is worth a go; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Low-mileage 3yo who may still have further progress in him; enters calculations
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Lxvi And P Demilow

runners.name: Sandret
runners.spotlight: Veteran who has been ridden by Rebecca Hitchen since joining current yard, posting form figures of 31216; hindered by wide trip in AW contest on reappearance most recently; could go well with that outing under his belt.
runners.comment: Veteran who was hindered by wide trip in AW contest on reappearance

runners.name: Tees George
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mostly at up to 8.5f and has seemed ideally suited by Beverley/Newcastle, having registered all of his top-two finishes at those venues; doesn't look the answer in this scenario.
runners.comment: Doesn't look the answer over this trip and at this venue

runners.name: Princess Niyla
runners.spotlight: Has largely respectable form this term, most recently fifth to Shimmering Sands over C&D; weighted to get closer to that rival on the revised terms and is on an attractive mark; still seeking first handicap win away from Beverley but can't be dismissed.
runners.comment: Ties in with Shimmering Sands on C&D running last time; on an attractive mark

runners.name: Yermanthere
runners.spotlight: Prevailed narrowly at Ripon (1m2f, good) last time, recording first win since 2024; no spring chicken, being an 8yo, but he's technically still unexposed over this trip and remains well treated on historical data; should remain competitive.
runners.comment: Prevailed narrowly at Ripon last time; remains well treated on historical data

runners.name: Wrist Art
runners.spotlight: Has performed respectably in last two attempts under Evie Lee, finishing third both times, including latest start; however, continues on a long losing run, partly exemplified by being 0-14 for current stable.
runners.comment: Continues on a long losing run; 0-14 for current stable

races.race_name: Danny O'Neill Birthday "Confined" Maiden Stakes (No More Than Two Runs) (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Sea And Sun
runners.spotlight: Stablemate of newcomer Camelot Champion; showed some promise with second of seven in 1m3f event at Ripon in April; ran to an ordinary RPR, having finished 11l behind the odds-on winner who set the standard, but looks open to improvement.
runners.comment: Showed some promise with 11l second in Ripon event; looks open to improvement

runners.name: Camelot Champion
runners.spotlight: 50,000euros yearling; by Camelot; sibling to five winners, notably Luxembourg (1m-1m4f including Group 1; RPR 124); dam maiden (71); stablemate of once-raced Sea And Sun; respected newcomer, especially if the market speaks positively.
runners.comment: 50,000euros yearling; siblings include Group 1 winner Luxembourg; respected newcomer

runners.name: Alfred Wincham
runners.spotlight: Unraced for Godolphin; won going away, looking a useful sort, in good-ground bumper at Down Royal in sole start for Anthony McCann; sold for £80,000 later in May; this son of Dubawi is an interesting recruit who could do well on the Flat for his new yard.
runners.comment: Winner of an Irish bumper; this son of Dubawi could do well on the Flat for new yard

runners.name: Who's The Goat
runners.spotlight: Decorated Knight half-brother to three winners, notably Golden Wolf (1m2f; RPR 96); dam maiden (68), out of unraced sister to top stayer Yeats; makes belated debut aged six; probably best watched unless the betting suggests otherwise.
runners.comment: Makes belated debut aged six; best watched unless the betting suggests otherwise

runners.name: Knight Of Storms
runners.spotlight: Brings the worst form among the runners who have racecourse experience, having trailed home last of 11 in 1m2f maiden at Newbury; however, may post a better effort in this weaker race with the outing under his belt.
runners.comment: Trailed home last of 11 in 1m2f maiden at Newbury

races.race_name: Darley EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Capichera
runners.spotlight: Has made the frame in all four outings and the form stacks up well; good second, with a few subsequent winners behind her, in Nottingham handicap (1m) most recently; subsequently withdrawn from Listed event due to soft ground; sets a rock-solid standard.
runners.comment: Has made the frame in all four runs; the form stacks up well; sets a rock-solid standard

runners.name: Take A View
runners.spotlight: Related to several winners for her connections; has made the frame in 1m2f contests at Newbury and Salisbury, while shaping as if this drop in distance is likely to suit; addition of tongue-tie may also contribute to a materialisation of progress; one of the main players.
runners.comment: Combination of drop in trip and first-time tongue-tie may prompt progress; possibilities

runners.name: Stolen Kisses
runners.spotlight: Twice-raced 4yo who brings the worst form having registered RPRs in the 30s; handicaps should be more suitable shortly.
runners.comment: Brings the worst form

runners.name: Carnival Of Light
runners.spotlight: 4yo filly who finished fourth of six in 1m event at Ripon where she was the only newcomer in the field; ran to an ordinary RPR but may do better with the outing/experience under her belt.
runners.comment: Ran to ordinary RPR in Ripon event but may do better with the outing under her belt

runners.name: Sedeirah
runners.spotlight: Never dangerous but showed a bit of promise in making some late headway in 1m contest at Haydock, finishing sixth of eight; may take a step forward with the experience under her belt.
runners.comment: Made some late headway in 1m contest at Haydock; may take a step forward

races.race_name: Napoleons Casino Bradford Handicap

runners.name: Garden Oasis
runners.spotlight: Front-runner; best form this term when placed a few times in spring, showing that he retains plenty of ability aged 11; gained last win off 7lb higher over C&D and can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Front-running veteran who gained last win off 7lb higher over C&D; not ruled out

runners.name: Jez Bomb
runners.spotlight: Eight-time winner; has come up well short in big fields at York a couple of times of late; otherwise a consistent sort and has frame possibilities off current mark in this scenario.
runners.comment: Couple of recent efforts are perhaps best forgiven; consistent sort otherwise

runners.name: Selection
runners.spotlight: 4yo filly whose form at Pontefract comprises her sole turf win, gained over 6f in 2024, and strong-finishing second (nearly got up) over 1m last time; major player back up just 1lb.
runners.comment: Strong-finishing second (nearly got up) over C&D last time; major player back up just 1lb

runners.name: Sir Paul Ramsey
runners.spotlight: Well treated on the form he showed last August, including win at Haydock and close third at the York Ebor festival; absent since finishing rather tamely over C&D two months ago.
runners.comment: Well treated on the form he showed last August; absent since C&D defeat two months ago

runners.name: Dovey Moon
runners.spotlight: Record of 4-9 in 1m events on turf, most recently scoring at Salisbury on penultimate start; well held in competitive 1m2f event latest; drop back to ideal trip is the angle with him.
runners.comment: Drop back to ideal trip is the angle with him; 4-9 in 1m events on turf

runners.name: Kaleido
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner for previous yard; not particularly solid on his handicap form this term for new stable (40-1 last time) but is the type to bear in mind if there's market support.
runners.comment: Not solid on his form for new yard but is one to bear in mind if there's market support

runners.name: Sunriseontheboyne
runners.spotlight: Won his final start in Ireland but hasn't been in anything like the same form for new stable; best watched unless the betting suggests a revival is likely.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish 4yo whose form for new yard is far from convincing
runners.owner: Future Champions Racing Syn & Ben Haslam

runners.name: Cosmos Raj
runners.spotlight: 12-time winner; creditable second in this race in 2022 and 2025 but on both occasions he was in better form at the time; out of sorts since his last success.
runners.comment: Creditable second in this race in 2022 and 2025; out of sorts since last win

races.race_name: Pontefract Ladies Day Wednesday 5th August Handicap

runners.name: Queen Sana
runners.spotlight: Maiden who has proved largely consistent and is knocking on the door, having posted form figures of 222 since switched to turf; ran well in 3yo handicap over C&D (good) most recently; solid claims.
runners.comment: Maiden who is knocking at the door; good second over C&D most recently; solid claims

runners.name: Lucius Aurelius
runners.spotlight: Seems ideally suited by Catterick, the scene of his April win and bronze medal latest start; has an unconvincing record at Pontefract; new headgear needs to make a difference.
runners.comment: Seems ideally suited by Catterick; has an unconvincing record at Pontefract
runners.owner: John Kelly, John McNeill & Kevin Mowatt

runners.name: Betweenthesticks
runners.spotlight: Scored narrowly off 1lb lower over C&D when completing an April double; unable to sustain a good start to the campaign, having shown a dip in form the last twice.
runners.comment: Scored over C&D to complete an April double; form has dipped on last two starts
runners.owner: J A Swinburne & Mrs Ruth A Carr

runners.name: Knicks
runners.spotlight: Latest effort suggests he remains in form; still on a long losing sequence but looked an unlucky third, having been forced to switch, over C&D on penultimate start; possibilities off same mark.
runners.comment: Unlucky third, having been forced to switch, over C&D on penultimate start; possibilities

runners.name: Soca Star
runners.spotlight: Similar type to Queen Sana, being a 3yo maiden who has proved consistent in the main; interesting on penultimate effort, which has been strongly franked by the improving winner since.
runners.comment: Largely consistent maiden; interesting on penultimate effort (form strongly boosted since)

runners.name: Without Flaw
runners.spotlight: Not quite in top form this term, beaten favourite at Thirsk most recently; went close over C&D last autumn but her ideal level is Class 6, as a record of 0-18 in higher grades reflects.
runners.comment: Went close over C&D last autumn but her ideal level is Class 6

runners.name: Hover On The Wind
runners.spotlight: Attractively treated, having gained all wins off current mark or higher; has rather lacked consistency this year but holds a good chance provided he turns up in top form.
runners.comment: Has rather lacked consistency this year but is attractively treated on best form

runners.name: Dream Deal
runners.spotlight: Prevailed at Beverley (good) last time, recording a first win away from Catterick and first over 5f; proved suited by the belated drop back in distance and may remain competitive; close second in this race in 2023.
runners.comment: Prevailed at Beverley last time, seeming suited by the belated drop back to 5f

races.race_name: Wilfred Underwood Memorial Handicap

runners.name: Lady Bouquet
runners.spotlight: Has made the frame twice since dropped back to 5f, both times shaping as if this return to 6f is likely to suit; indeed, she gained most recent win over this trip; close fourth at Pontefract latest; in the mix.
runners.comment: Close fourth at Pontefract last time; in the mix

runners.name: Superstorm
runners.spotlight: 3yo maiden who has broadly respectable 7f form this term; gives the impression this return to 6f is worth exploring; possibilities off a mark that's 7lb below his opening one.
runners.comment: 3yo maiden who may benefit from giving 6f another go

runners.name: Ganthorpe
runners.spotlight: Modest 3yo who has a peak RPR of 48; managed to open his account in Carlisle event (6f) on penultimate start but wasn't in the same form last time; seems to have limitations.
runners.comment: Seems to have limitations, despite being a recent winner

runners.name: Ideal Guest
runners.spotlight: Losing run is mounting up but he shaped well from the front over C&D (good) eight days ago, eventually tying up having kicked clear too soon in hindsight, beaten only 2l; big chance off same mark with Ethan Tindall taking off a bonus 5lb, provided his energy is used better.
runners.comment: Shaped well from the front over C&D eight days ago; respected

runners.name: Full Gas
runners.spotlight: 3yo maiden who failed to settle when back over 7f most recently; performed encouragingly in last two 6f attempts, making the frame at Newcastle (AW) and Haydock; could go well.
runners.comment: 3yo maiden; made the frame in last two 6f attempts

runners.name: Great Success
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden who has inconsistent form but posted one of his better efforts last time; sole attempt over C&D (on debut) resulted in his peak form; interesting back here.
runners.comment: Sole attempt over C&D (on debut) resulted in his peak effort

runners.name: Kyber Crystal
runners.spotlight: Has done her winning over 5f, most recently off 4lb higher at Pontefract last September (inconsistent since); attractively treated but has something to prove back up in trip.
runners.comment: Has gained all wins over 5f; something to prove back at 6f

runners.name: Big Apple Jack
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden and balance of form this season doesn't strongly suggest he'll defy top weight in this field; on a positive note, he's nicely treated on peak 3yo efforts.
runners.comment: Still a maiden but is nicely treated on peak 3yo efforts

races.race_name: Wayne Conway Memorial Handicap

runners.name: Alice's Influence
runners.spotlight: 3yo filly who initially improved for the fitting of a visor, dead-heating at Wetherby then nose second at Bath; has regressed in two starts since but may stage a turnaround with the headgear, which has evidently stopped working, now removed.
runners.comment: Effects of visor have soon worn off; may stage a turnaround with the headgear removed

runners.name: Daring Leader
runners.spotlight: Scored off lower marks on turf and AW last term; inconsistent this season but is interesting on his penultimate effort, namely third at Ripon behind a pair of subsequent scorers.
runners.comment: Inconsistent this term but is interesting on penultimate effort (form franked)
runners.owner: D J Jones & M T Brougham

runners.name: Far Ahead
runners.spotlight: Novice winner who is 0-12 in handicaps but has shown positive signs with second-place finishes at Beverley (behind Anificas Beauty on worse terms) and Hamilton the last twice, despite taking a good hold both times; possibilities, especially if settling better.
runners.comment: 0-12 in handicaps but has shown positive signs the last twice; possibilities
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: Hostelry
runners.spotlight: Ten-time winner; kept on for never-dangerous fifth, beaten under 3l, when favourite for the latest Beverley race won by Anificas Beauty; closely matched with that rival on the revised terms.
runners.comment: Ten-time winner who ties in with Anificas Beauty on latest Beverley running

runners.name: Captain Bruce
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner for previous yard; has achieved little on turf, latterly in two starts for new stable; enough to prove.
runners.comment: Dual AW winner who still has something to prove on turf

runners.name: Shark Two One
runners.spotlight: Placed in sole 1m attempt, raced over shorter otherwise; ran respectably last time in a Leicester classified event he'd landed 12 months earlier; hasn't won a handicap since 2023.
runners.comment: Placed in sole 1m attempt, raced at shorter otherwise; hasn't won a handicap since 2023

runners.name: Elettaria
runners.spotlight: Ran respectably in this contest last year (finished fifth) and at Beverley latest start; however, this three-time Newcastle AW winner is only 1-26 on turf, the success registered in 2023.
runners.comment: Respectable fifth in this race last year but it's a long time since her sole turf win

runners.name: Nakatomi
runners.spotlight: Gave impression something was possibly amiss last time; remains a longstanding maiden but his resurgent May efforts (finished third then close second) suggested a belated first win may be near.
runners.comment: Longstanding maiden but his May efforts suggested a belated first win may be near
runners.owner: S & S Kyriakides, K Mount & F Reay

runners.name: Anificas Beauty
runners.spotlight: Prevailed by 1l last time to complete a Beverley double; back in good form but she failed in two previous hat-trick attempts and was beaten off 2lb lower (finished fourth) in this race 12 months ago.
runners.comment: Prevailed last time to complete a Beverley double; failed in two previous hat-trick bids

races.race_name: Watch On Racing TV Juvenile Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Tiny Riot
runners.spotlight: Modest form in four Flat runs but he's done well switched to hurdling recently, winning at Hexham (2m, good) before finishing runner-up behind a useful prospect at Market Rasen (2m, good); sets the standard on that latest form and he's open to more progress for top yard; key player.
runners.comment: Won on hurdling debut at Hexham before a creditable second at Market Rasen; key player

runners.name: High Aura
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to winners Alpine Oasis (1m/1m4f AW; RPR 84), Albion Princess (1m-10.6f; 74), Lillistar (2m AW; 63) and Showstorm (German 9.5f); dam 1m1f-1m6f winner (89), out of Listed-placed 1m winner; stablemate of Tiny Riot and market guide on her debut.
runners.comment: Half-sister to Flat winners; stablemate of Tiny Riot and market should guide on debut

runners.name: Lucky Sevens
runners.spotlight: Made it 2-9 on the Flat when landing a Brighton handicap (1m2f, good to firm) last Sunday; that was a Class 6 event but he's a half-brother to four hurdle winners and should have a future at this new discipline; needs a close look in market.
runners.comment: Dual Flat winner and he's a half-brother to four hurdle winners; needs close a look

runners.name: Serragasso
runners.spotlight: Big prices and he's hasn't shown a great deal in four Flat runs since December including a Southwell handicap (7f, AW); best watched on hurdling debut.
runners.comment: Hasn't shown much in four Flat runs and he's best watched on hurdling debut

runners.name: Wicksey
runners.spotlight: Won three in a row on AW (1m2f/1m4f) in February and he was a creditable second when upped to 1m6f in a Redcar handicap (good to firm) last month; takes on two previous winners on this hurdling debut but he has some fair form on the Flat and he's not ruled out.
runners.comment: Triple winner on the Flat and he ran well at 1m6f latest; not ruled out on hurdling debut

runners.name: Sudbury Hill
runners.spotlight: Fairly useful on the Flat at his best; held in second when odds-on for hurdling debut at Hexham (2m, good) but he built on that when trouncing two rivals at Newton Abbot (2m1f, good) last week; could have a lot more to offer in this sphere and he's respected under a penalty.
runners.comment: Trounced two rivals at Newton Abbot last week and he's respected under a penalty

races.race_name: Visit racingtv.com Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Uhtred Ragnarson
runners.spotlight: Returned after six months off with a win in a Warwick handicap (2m, good) last November; looks interesting on that form but he fell on his final run during the winter and was tailed off on his Aintree reappearance last month; still has low mileage but he needs to bounce back near best.
runners.comment: Low-mileage 5yo but he has a patchy profile and needs to bounce back near best

runners.name: Playful Fox
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 4yo who was runner-up in a Worcester handicap (2m, good) last month and he had an excuse when upped to 2m4f there six days later; could resume his progress on this drop back in trip and he's an interesting contender for Dan Skelton.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo who had an excuse last time and he could kick on again back at this trip

runners.name: Reteti
runners.spotlight: 15-race maiden but he ran well in two hurdle handicaps last month including when runner-up over C&D (good to firm) on his penultimate start; only 1lb higher here and he should go well again.
runners.comment: 0-15 but he's run well in last two starts including when runner-up over C&D; in the mix

runners.name: Juan Bermudez
runners.spotlight: All of his four hurdle wins have been at Stratford and latest was a front-running success over about 2m3f (good to firm) last month; remains feasibly treated off 3lb higher and this in-form 7yo is a major player on this drop back in trip.
runners.comment: Stratford specialist who made all over about 2m3f here (good to firm) latest; major player

runners.name: Pahlavi
runners.spotlight: Won a handicap hurdle at Downpatrick (2m2f, good) last August but he's been out of sorts in three runs (chase/hurdle) this spring and overall record is now 1-31; opposable.
runners.comment: Overall record of 1-31 and he's been out of sorts in three runs this spring; opposable

runners.name: Katherine
runners.spotlight: Ran surprisingly well behind the classy Murcia in a mares' hurdle at Listowel (2m, good) last month but she struggled back in handicap chase last time; now returns to hurdling but she's hard to predict and her last win was in October 2024; others are more convincing.
runners.comment: Her last win was in 2024 and she struggled in a handicap chase last time; down the list

races.race_name: Sun Racing The Home Of Racing Novices' Handicap Chase

runners.name: A Little Something
runners.spotlight: 0-8 over hurdles but she's been a big improver since switched to chasing and completed a double with an emphatic win at Fontwell (2m3f, good) 19 days ago; now 11lb higher but she's on a roll and could continue on an upward curve over fences; key player.
runners.comment: Made it 2-5 over fences with an easy win at Fontwell; big player again off 11lb higher

runners.name: Catchim
runners.spotlight: Record of 1-14 over fences but that win came at Market Rasen (2m1f, good) in April and he was a front-running second behind a major improver at Southwell (2m, good) last time; this is a different test but his hurdles win was over 2m4f and he still looks feasibly treated; interesting contender.
runners.comment: Runner-up behind a major improver at Southwell latest and he's respected back up in trip

runners.name: Gore Point
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner who has reached the frame in all of his four chase runs; was no match for A Little Something when runner-up at Fontwell (2m3f, good) this month but that was after an absence and he has a big swing in the weights with that rival; could be dangerous.
runners.comment: Was no match for A Little Something at Fontwell but he has a big swing in the weights here

runners.name: White Riot
runners.spotlight: Good-ground bumper winner but he's failed to complete in two of his four hurdle runs and was tailed off at Uttoxeter last time; still early days but he has something to prove and needs a transformation on handicap/chase debut; tongue-tie removed.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 6yo but he needs a transformation on his handicap/chase debut

runners.name: No Mean Feat
runners.spotlight: Ran respectably when fourth over hurdles at Fontwell (2m3f, good) last month; unexposed over fences but he remains a maiden after 17 starts under rules and needs to find more back in this sphere; had wind surgery since his last run.
runners.comment: Unexposed over fences but he's still a maiden and needs to find more  after wind surgery

runners.name: Kings Order
runners.spotlight: Maiden who has form figures of 0FP706 over hurdles and was a remote fifth at a big prices on his recent chase debut at Worcester (2m4f, good); has a lot to prove and he needs a tongue-tie to make a big difference.
runners.comment: No joy over hurdles and was a remote sixth on recent chase debut at Worcester; passed over

runners.name: Citizen Jane
runners.spotlight: Triple point winner who scored in that sphere on her penultimate run; now switches to regulation fences but she's 0-12 over hurdles and this looks tough from out of the weights.
runners.comment: Triple point winner but she's 0-12 over hurdles; 3lb out of weights on belated chase debut

races.race_name: Racing TV Profits Returned To Racing Novices' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Slack Alice
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the seventh attempt over hurdles when justifying support at Market Rasen (2m7f, good) 17 days ago; up 7lb but that was an emphatic success and she's open to more progress; big player.
runners.comment: Off the mark with a clearcut win at Market Rasen (2m7f, good); big player up 7lb

runners.name: Back To Cali
runners.spotlight: 0-8 over hurdles in Ireland but he finished third of 20 in a Cork handicap (2m, yielding) in April on his final run; left Shark Hanlon for 24,000euros since; now tries a new trip after a break but he needs a close look on his first run Dan Skelton.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish; changed hands for 24,000euros; needs a close look on first run for Dan Skelton

runners.name: French Diablo
runners.spotlight: Well held in his first two runs and was pulled up in a maiden at Newton Abbot (2m5f, good) on his recent return; now goes handicapping but he needs improvement to make an impact from out of the weights.
runners.comment: Very lightly raced 4yo but he needs improvement on handicap debut; 2lb out of the weights

runners.name: Alex The Great
runners.spotlight: Three-time AW winner (1m6f-2m) but he's been well held in four hurdle runs since April including a Worcester handicap (2m, good; 33-1); this stiffer stamina test could help but he needs to show a lot more.
runners.comment: Still unexposed over hurdles but he needs improvement upped to 2m6f

runners.name: Mc'flo
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden who has finished down the field in her five handicaps and was 13l behind Slack Alice at Market Rasen (2m7f, good) last time; passed over.
runners.comment: Ten-race maiden who has yet to show any worthwhile form over hurdles; passed over

runners.name: Vesalius
runners.spotlight: Well held in his first three hurdle runs before being pulled up on handicap debut at Bangor (2m3f, good) last month; that was after an absence but he needs cheekpieces to make a big difference on this step back up in trip.
runners.comment: Unexposed over hurdles but he needs cheekpieces to make a big difference back up in trip

runners.name: North Account
runners.spotlight: Struggled at massive prices in three hurdles runs last year and he unseated when favourite in a point on his return last month; still early days but he needs a transformation on this switch to a handicap.
runners.comment: Unexposed 6yo but he needs a transformation on handicap debut

races.race_name: Every Race Live On Racing TV Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: She Is For Me Boys
runners.spotlight: Point and dual hurdle winner who made 2-7 in fences when landing the odds in a mares' handicap at Worcester (2m4f, good; tongue-tie added) 23 days ago; jumped well under a positive ride in that race and she's a big player off only 2lb higher here.
runners.comment: Jumped well when making all at Worcester and she's only 2lb higher here; big player

runners.name: Nickelforce
runners.spotlight: Made it 4-15 over fences when forging clear to win by 9l at Fakenham (2m5f, good) last month; that was in a small field but he hit a personal best with that success and a 4lb rise looks fair enough; respected.
runners.comment: Hit a personal best when winning at Fakenham and a 4lb rise looks fair enough; big player

runners.name: Bagheera Ginge
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who returned from seven-month break with 7l win in first-time visor over C&D (good to firm) last month; only 3lb higher than for that success but he failed to repeat the form when a remote fifth at Worcester (2m7f, good); that was another blip in his patchy profile and he needs to bounce back again.
runners.comment: Reappeared with a win over C&D last month but he was tailed off at Worcester last time

runners.name: Normofthenorth
runners.spotlight: 12yo who recorded his tenth point when scoring on his comeback at Kingston Blount (3m, good) last month; this is his only his third run under rules but his first two resulted in a win and second in hunter chases and latest was over C&D; could go well back in this sphere and he's not ruled out on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Ten-time point winner and he's not ruled out back over regulation fences

races.race_name: Racing Again Tomorrow Evening Mares' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Callin Baton Rouge
runners.spotlight: Not quite at her best this year but she was placed at Huntingdon (2m, good to firm) on her penultimate run and is now 6lb lower than for her win at Uttoxeter last summer; could go well back in trip and she's not ruled out.
runners.comment: Well handicapped on her win at Uttoxeter last summer and she's not ruled out back in trip

runners.name: Best Night
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdles winner who returned to form with a second at Warwick (2m, good) 26 days ago, and that was behind a major improver who has won seven in a row since the end of May; on same mark as for that last run and she's a big player if she can back that up.
runners.comment: Back to form when runner-up behind a quadruple subsequent winner at Warwick; interesting

runners.name: Jukebox Annie
runners.spotlight: Sole hurdle win was in a Taunton novice (2m, good to firm) in November 2024; generally struggled last year but she reappeared with an encouraging fourth at Newton Abbot (2m1f, good) this month; should get a decent tow into this race and has each-way claims if she can build on that last run.
runners.comment: Signs of revival when fourth at Newton Abbot and has claims if she can build on that

runners.name: Northern Rose
runners.spotlight: Runner-up four times last year but she reappeared with a tailed-off effort over C&D (good) 13 days ago and is now 0-24 over hurdles; others are more convincing.
runners.comment: 0-24 over hurdles and was tailed off on her reappearance over C&D 13 days ago

runners.name: A Tickatickatiming
runners.spotlight: Her last win was in a Plumpton maiden in November 2023; mixed results over hurdles this year but was runner-up at Plumpton (2m, soft) in February and she finished a fair fourth at Fontwell (2m3f, good) 19 days ago; continues to edge down the weights and she could be in the mix back in trip.
runners.comment: On dangerous mark and was a fair fourth at Fontwell last time; could be in the mix

runners.name: Never Told Me
runners.spotlight: Showed promise in maiden hurdles last summer and she ran well for a long way before falling at the last on her reappearance at Fakenham (2m, good; first run after wind surgery) last month; still unexposed after only six starts and she needs watching in market on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Showed promise in maidens and she needs watching in market on handicap debut

runners.name: Basilette
runners.spotlight: Completed a double over hurdles at Fakenham (2m, good soft) last October and she's done well back on the Flat this spring including a win on firm ground at Bath (11.6f); her last hurdle win was off 6lb lower but that was a comfortable success and she's in the mix back in this sphere.
runners.comment: Two hurdle wins last autumn and she's scored on the Flat this spring; in the mix

runners.name: Shing Shilla
runners.spotlight: Third in a Sligo handicap (2m2f, good) last August but she's not gone on from that and has been pulled up and tailed off in last two starts; overall record is now 0-17 and others are preferred,
runners.comment: 17-race maiden and she's been pulled up and tailed off in last two starts; passed over

runners.name: Little Lady Lucy
runners.spotlight: Turned things around when scoring at Wincanton (1m7f, good) in April and she followed up under a penalty Taunton (2m, good); came up a bit short in hat-trick bid over C&D (good to firm) last month but that was another good effort and she still looks on a workable mark; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Two wins in April and was a good second over C&D (good to firm) latest; shortlisted

runners.name: Lucy The Wire
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 4yo who won at Leicester (1m7f, soft) last autumn and she bounced back with a close call in a Kempton handicap (2m, good; sustained a wound to her left fore) in March; there was little separating the first three last time but she's on same mark here and is key player on her return.
runners.comment: Unexposed 4yo who went close at Kempton in March and is on same mark here; key player

runners.name: Driving Miss d'Azy
runners.spotlight: Beaten 21l or more in all five runs including a Huntingdon handicap (2m, good to firm) on her reappearance last month; still unexposed but she needs improvement to get involved.
runners.comment: Well held in all five runs including a Huntingdon handicap; needs improvement

runners.name: Roarin' Success
runners.spotlight: Useful Flat handicapper who showed promise in maiden hurdles but she was beaten 28l in a Uttoxeter handicap (2m, good) last month; still early days in this sphere but she has bit to prove after that last run; tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Still early days over hurdles but she needs improvement with tongue-tie added
runners.owner: Hale Racing, Lyle Farming Company & Ptnr

runners.name: Miss Fedora
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle and dual chase winner when trained by Seamus Mullins; flopped over fences (pulled up) on stable debut in March and was well held back over hurdles at Taunton last time; well handicapped on her best Irish form but she needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: Well handicapped on her best Irish form but she needs to get back on track

runners.name: Hollysaway
runners.spotlight: Tailed off at massive prices in three novice/maiden hurdles since March; now goes handicapping but she makes no appeal from a long way out of the weights.
runners.comment: Tailed off in all three runs and is a long way out of the weights on handicap debut

races.race_name: Dandara EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: This Moment
runners.spotlight: Plenty to like about his debut second at Hamilton, when well clear of the rest having run green early; ran to a similar level at Chester one month ago and sets the standard on either effort.
runners.comment: Run to similar level in two starts and sets the standard on either effort; the one to beat
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Rogue Imperial
runners.spotlight: 170,000 breeze-up purchase who built on a promising debut when third at Lingfield to two who went on to run well at Royal Ascot last time; rates the main threat to This Moment.
runners.comment: Two fair runs, the latest when 3rd at Lingfield to two who ran well at Ascot since

runners.name: High Calibre
runners.spotlight: Started a short price on his Brighton debut in a weak-looking maiden one month ago, when a remote last of five; everything went wrong that day, with him being green and ill at ease round the undulations, and he's obviously felt capable of better.
runners.comment: Short price on his Brighton debut; everything went wrong that day and should fare better

runners.name: Midgham Man
runners.spotlight: Gelded half-brother to two 6f winners for the yard (out of a 6f winner), one who went close on debut; trainer 4-24 with 2yos on this track; worth a market check.
runners.comment: Half-brother to two 6f winners for yard; trainer 4-24 with 2yos on this track

runners.name: Soldier Of The Sea
runners.spotlight: Early-foaled 130,000euros yearling, 100,000euros breeze-up 2yo; stable just getting going with juveniles for the season (had a newcomer win at Ripon recently) and the market will show what's expected.
runners.comment: Early-foaled 130,000euros yearling, 100,000euros breeze-up 2yo; market will guide

runners.name: Sea Palace
runners.spotlight: Well-related 120,000gns yearling who finished a good way off the front pair when easy to back on his debut at Hamilton (6f, good to soft) this month; needs improvement.
runners.comment: Well-related 120,000gns yearling; good way off the front pair when easy to back on debut

runners.name: Percy Shaw
runners.spotlight: Started a big price when well held in a Newbury novice recently; trainer a pleasing 6-25 with 2yos here down the seasons but this one will need a big step forward.
runners.comment: Started a big price when well held in a Newbury novice recently; needs a big step forward

races.race_name: Dandara Golwg Gwendraeth Handicap

runners.name: Tickettothestars
runners.spotlight: No excuses off her revised mark since a Nottingham win off 4lb lower (well drawn at Lingfield; some way below that level at Bath latest); completely exposed now.
runners.comment: No excuses off her revised mark since a Nottingham win off 4lb lower; completely exposed

runners.name: Beelzebub
runners.spotlight: Tumbled down the weights for three yards on the AW and took advantage of a lenient turf mark for his new trainer, without the usual headgear, under Myla Coppins at Windsor last Monday; had a good bit in hand there and has no penalty for it; obvious chance.
runners.comment: Took advantage of a lenient turf mark for his new trainer last Monday; no penalty today

runners.name: In Denial
runners.spotlight: Fair turf debut in April, when third to Tickettothestars (5lb better off today), but didn't back it up last time; did get in a bumping match early, though, and it's too soon to write her off.
runners.comment: Fair turf debut, when third to Tickettothestars (5lb better off); didn't back it up latest

runners.name: Jackie Brown
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish mare who was all the rage for a Bath 0-50 classified event for her new yard early in the month, despite having a bit to find at the weights; didn't appear to have any excuses there but is better off back into handicap company.
runners.comment: All the rage for a Bath 0-50 classified event for her new yard early in the month; claims

runners.name: Lieutenant Sir
runners.spotlight: Fair turf/handicap debut on his return from six months off on first run since being gelded; entitled to have come on for that and it's still early days with him.
runners.comment: Fair turf/handicap debut on his return from six months off; still early days with him

runners.name: Outer Edge
runners.spotlight: Went close second time out as a 3yo and won on his second start last season, which bodes well off the back of a pleasing Windsor comeback five weeks ago; needs considering at this lower level off a career-low mark.
runners.comment: Went close second time out as a 3yo and won on his second start last season; considered
runners.owner: Int Plywood (lmporters) Ltd And C Mason

races.race_name: Diplomat Hotel Handicap

runners.name: Zighy
runners.spotlight: Wins have come over 6f on Tapeta, latterly off 6lb lower; now has her first 5f run since beaten at odds-on on her 2yo debut but she showed good toe to make most round Chester last time before picked off by two who'd sat in the rear and this mark looks within range.
runners.comment: Good toe to make most at Chester (6f) last time before picked off; mark looks within range
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: El Bufalo
runners.spotlight: Done his winning on the AW but is fine on turf; not best drawn when behind All Ways Glamorous at Ascot in the spring (now 2lb worse off) before not looking to see out 6f last time; needs a career-best off his current mark but this is less competitive.
runners.comment: Done his winning on the AW but is fine on turf; needs a career-best off his current mark

runners.name: Comic Strip
runners.spotlight: Held since winning a couple of nurseries on Tapeta last backend, latterly off 3lb lower; looked to find the sixth furlong beyond him last time but he has to prove this mark is within range on turf.
runners.comment: Held since winning a couple of nurseries on Tapeta last backend; happier back down from 6f

runners.name: All Ways Glamorous
runners.spotlight: Progressive last year and as good as ever when third on his Ascot comeback in the spring; pulled hard in first-time cheekpieces (removed today) last time, an effort that looks best ignored, and he's one of the likelier winners back from a short break.
runners.comment: Latest effort best ignored and he's one of the likelier winners back from a short break

runners.name: Rogue Enforcer
runners.spotlight: Has never run on ground faster than good (and taken out before on account of unsuitable good ground), so conditions could be a worry; hard to place after a Listed win in Italy last autumn ruined his mark and has struggled in three runs for this yard.
runners.comment: Never run on going faster than good; hard to place after a Listed win in Italy last autumn

races.race_name: Dress For The Occasions Handicap

runners.name: Norfolk Blue
runners.spotlight: Hit traffic at Windsor (first-time cheekpieces) last month and built on that back down to 7f last time; been put up 3lb for it but this is less competitive and he's high on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Been put up 3lb for his latest second but this is less competitive; high on the shortlist

runners.name: Quick Quasar
runners.spotlight: Free-going gelding who was pestered in front and didn't see out 8.7f on Tapeta last time, having previously won easily (7f) off 9lb lower; drop back in trip will suit on his turf debut.
runners.comment: Pestered in front, didn't see out 8.7f on Tapeta last time; better expected on turf debut

runners.name: Flyta
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to five turf winners who bettered her AW efforts when second at a big price at Windsor (1m) last time; didn't look to fail through lack of stamina but it gave something to build on.
runners.comment: Half-sister to five turf winners who bettered her AW efforts latest; one to consider

runners.name: Twilight Glow
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent maiden who faded late on his first run at beyond 7f when behind Flyta at Windsor a fortnight ago; much will hinge on how he responds to first-time headgear down another 3lb.
runners.comment: Inconsistent maiden; much will hinge on how he responds to first-time headgear down 3lb

runners.name: Victory Sound
runners.spotlight: Useful 2yo for Charles Hills who proved hard to place last year and tumbled down the weights; found his level again now, just cracking late over 1m last time, and won't mind the return to turf.
runners.comment: Found his level again, just cracking late over 1m last time; won't mind the return to turf

runners.name: Strong Voice
runners.spotlight: Inclined to hang left a couple of times, including when well beaten on his turf debut/comeback in the spring; since undergone wind surgery and the market should give some clues as to what's been made of his opening mark.
runners.comment: Had wind surgery; market should give some clues as to what's been made of his opening mark

runners.name: Mayberry Moon
runners.spotlight: Wins for Michael Dods came on soft (2yo debut) and good to soft, and he hung left both runs on good to firm; 4lb higher than his last winning mark and returns from ten months off, having been picked up for 1,000gns; hard to find many positives.
runners.comment: Picked up for 1,000gns; 4lb above his last winning mark; off for ten months

races.race_name: Preventapest Handicap

runners.name: Port Louis
runners.spotlight: Clearly happier on turf than the AW but is handicapped accordingly and was in no kind of form in the spring; the returning cheekpieces need to induce a revival.
runners.comment: Happier on turf than the AW but handicapped accordingly; in no kind of form in the spring

runners.name: Pulsar Star
runners.spotlight: Maiden who turned in a solid handicap debut in the spring before being drawn wide down to 7f at York; touch disappointing last time but this is less competitive back up in trip.
runners.comment: Remains a maiden; bit disappointing last time but this is less competitive back up in trip

runners.name: Orchard
runners.spotlight: Tended to stick to slow turf/Polytrack; missed a year after finishing lame last June and was entitled to have needed his comeback, when third to Grey Soul over C&D; hit traffic late that day and is 2lb better off, so there's a fair chance he turns things round.
runners.comment: Third to Grey Soul over C&D on comeback from a year off; fair chance of turning it round

runners.name: Grey Soul
runners.spotlight: Blinkers did the trick when winning a Tapeta handicap for Roger Varian last autumn and he made it 2-2 in headgear off 3lb lower over C&D (good to soft; cheekpieces) early in the month; been taken out on account of good to firm going before; player otherwise.
runners.comment: Made it 2-2 in headgear off 3lb lower over C&D (good to soft) early in the month; player

runners.name: Port Erin
runners.spotlight: Won three over this trip last year, twice from the front on turf, latterly off a 3lb lower mark; well held in two runs back but this is less competitive under Billy Loughnane and he may well be able to get his own way in this smaller field; possibilities if so.
runners.comment: Won three over 1m last year, twice from the front on turf; player if able to dictate

runners.name: Bold Impact
runners.spotlight: Looked to find this trip beyond him last month but worth another try over it on latest Chepstow evidence (7f, soft); on a workable mark again now, having missed a lot of last year for Ralph Beckett.
runners.comment: On workable mark again now, having missed a lot of last year for Ralph Beckett; considered

races.race_name: Plan A Restricted Maiden Stakes (For Horses In Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Marquessofanglesey
runners.spotlight: Steady progression on the AW, latterly when one of three to come away (8.7f; third a winner since); goes up in trip on his turf debut and sets the standard under Billy Loughnane.
runners.comment: Steady progression on the AW; goes up in trip on his turf debut and sets the standard
runners.owner: Julie Martin & David R Martin & Partner

runners.name: Round The Table
runners.spotlight: Well supported for debut in a Newbury maiden that's worked out well but needed the experience, pulling hard early and lugging left before finishing nicely; will have learned plenty from that and holds leading claims.
runners.comment: Needed debut, pulling hard early and lugging left before finishing nicely; leading claims

runners.name: Parc Dosne
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to the yard's 1m2f Royal Ascot winner (good to firm) Quai De Bethune; may not have appreciated very soft ground on his debut early in the month and market useful for last year's successful trainer.
runners.comment: May not have appreciated very soft ground on his debut early in the month; market useful

runners.name: Le Morbihan
runners.spotlight: Latest effort at Doncaster can be ignored, with him hanging badly right early in the straight and ending up on the stand rail; always the concern that he's tackling another left-handed course, though, while it's possible he was flattered from the front on debut.
runners.comment: Hung badly right at Doncaster; may have been flattered from the front on debut

runners.name: Seraglio Point
runners.spotlight: Not without hope in two runs in the spring for a yard that's had a couple of winners lately; will need a good step forward to take this, though, and is likely to be seen to better effect once handicapping.
runners.comment: Will need a good step forward to win; likely to be seen to better effect once handicapping

races.race_name: Dress For The Occasions Handicap

runners.name: Beaune
runners.spotlight: Won three on the AW earlier in the year; often ran well here last summer but off much lower marks and it may well be that the handicapper has closed in now.
runners.comment: Often ran well here last summer but off much lower marks; handicapper has closed in now

runners.name: Captain Cairney
runners.spotlight: 1m2f AW nursery winner last autumn who made a subsequent winner work for it on his first run since being gelded, and after a break, at Leicester three weeks ago; longer trip looks ideal and his claims are obvious off the same mark for a yard that does well here.
runners.comment: Solid comeback; longer trip looks ideal and his claims are obvious off the same mark
runners.owner: J O'Mulloy And W R Muir

runners.name: Buck Barrow
runners.spotlight: Likes it here and took advantage of the drop to 0-50 classified company when winning a weak race over C&D early in the month; that won't have hurt his confidence but this is a deal more competitive.
runners.comment: 0-50 C&D win recently won't have hurt confidence but this is a deal more competitive

runners.name: Worlington
runners.spotlight: Two expensive defeats since switched to turf, looking to have no excuses upped to this trip last time having bumped into one at Redcar in April; questions to answer going in a first-time tongue-tie/cheekpieces combination.
runners.comment: Two expensive defeats since switched to turf; questions to answer in first-time headgear

runners.name: Ataturk
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to the same connections' useful stayer Kyle Of Lochalsh; blew the start last time but starts handicap life at a lowly level and should be suited by today's stiffer test; better can be expected.
runners.comment: Half-brother to same connections' useful stayer Kyle Of Lochalsh; better can be expected

runners.name: Home Secretary
runners.spotlight: This gelding still looked raw when last of five on his recent handicap debut at Doncaster (1m4f, good to firm; 7-1), having been gelded; could well have more to offer down in class though.
runners.comment: Still looked raw when last of five on recent handicap debut; could well have more to offer

runners.name: Ibn Sirin
runners.spotlight: Big prices and well beaten in three runs over 1m last backend, since when he's been gelded; well related (half-brother to useful stayer Live Your Dream) and in good hands, but would want to see some support after eight months off.
runners.comment: Well related and in good hands, but would want to see some support after eight months off

runners.name: Calypso Bloom
runners.spotlight: Triple-figure prices for three runs on the AW (8.7f-1m3f), finishing last of four when last seen in April; couldn't be considered on her turf/handicap debut unless there's good support this time.
runners.comment: Triple-figure prices for three runs on the AW; hard to fancy on her turf/handicap debut

races.race_name: Ronald Fletcher Baker Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Sharp Move
runners.spotlight: Well beaten when 6-4 favourite on debut in May 2025 (11.5f, good to soft); sole race since when putting the record straight in maiden at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) in November, stretching 7l clear in the final 1f; useful prospect on that evidence and the one to beat in this field.
runners.comment: Stretched 7l clear at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) in November on second start; useful prospect

runners.name: Tropbien
runners.spotlight: Well-bred Frankel colt who was ninth of ten at Newbury (16-1) but kept on well when fourth of six in maiden at Sandown (again 1m2f on good; 15-2) 16 days ago; this longer trip should suit him well, so further improvement (which is needed) is on its way.
runners.comment: This longer trip can suit him well, so further improvement (which is needed) is on its way

runners.name: Hapiness
runners.spotlight: Similar fair form when beaten a neck in maiden at Chepstow (1m2f, good to soft) and third of five in novice at Thirsk (1m4f, good); good place chance but probably needs extra if she's to win this.
runners.comment: Fair form both starts; good place chance but probably needs extra if she's to win this

runners.name: Frank Stamper
runners.spotlight: Fair, four-race maiden on AW Flat for Jamie Osborne last year; runner-up in two of his eight hurdle races for Warren Greatrex but soundly beaten on last two outings, claimed for £6,000 from a seller in May on latest; best watched for now.
runners.comment: Fair, four-race maiden on AW Flat; claimed for £6,000 from a selling hurdle last month

runners.name: Arklan
runners.spotlight: 90,000gns foal, 34,000gns yearling by Palace Pier; sixth foal; half-brother to winners Motazzen (10.5f-1m6f Flat, RPR 90; 2m-2m5f hurdle) and Crystal Mer (2m/2m4f hurdle); dam French 1m3f and 1m4f (Listed) winner (107), half-sister to 1m2f Group 1 winner Matterhorn; faces notable opposition on his debut but needs a market check.
runners.comment: 34,000gns yearling by Palace Pier; has notable rivals on debut but needs a market check

runners.name: Far Far Out
runners.spotlight: Better here on second start (1m, good to firm) but still well held and modest form; sold out of Roger Varian's for 17,500gns three days later and has been gelded; best watched for now.
runners.comment: Modest form both starts; sold out of Roger Varian's for 17,500gns and gelded

runners.name: Ice To Meet You
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in a maiden at Lingfield (7f, good) and novice here (1m2f, good to firm); hooded in both but not today.
runners.comment: Tailed off in 7f maiden at Lingfield and 1m2f novice here; hooded in both but not today

runners.name: Sixty Plus
runners.spotlight: Fair hurdles form to win four in a row (2m2f-2m4f) last summer but lost his form in that sphere; debut under Flat rules when 17l sixth of ten in maiden at Goodwood (1m6f, good; 18-1) ten days ago, making most, which is something to build on but his turn on the Flat may not come just yet.
runners.comment: Fair hurdler last summer but lost his form; Flat debut ten days ago; best watched for now

runners.name: Digger One
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in his two bumpers and a 1m2f Windsor novice.
runners.comment: Well beaten in his two bumpers and a 1m2f Windsor novice

races.race_name: Catalyst Finance British EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Captaincy
runners.spotlight: 100,000gns yearling who looks bred for sprinting; 18-1, just over 4l third of eight in maiden at Leicester (6f, good to firm) five weeks ago was fair form; it sets the standard in this field and he should improve.
runners.comment: Leicester third (6f, good to firm) sets the standard in this field and he should improve

runners.name: Absolute Diamond
runners.spotlight: £12,000 breeze-up 2yo; 33-1 for six-runner novice at Chepstow (6f, good to soft) two weeks ago, but made most and battled on for second behind an impressive winner; should improve and has place chance again at least.
runners.comment: 33-1 for six-runner novice at Chepstow (6f, good to soft) but made most to be second

runners.name: Desert Legend
runners.spotlight: 8,000euros yearling, resold £35,000 yearling by Dubawi Legend; second foal; dam unplaced at 5f-6f (RPR 45), closely related to a US 8.5f stakes winner, out of 6f-winning half-sister to prolific 5f-7f winners Whitbarrow and Green Park; major yard runs two and he needs a close look.
runners.comment: Resold £35,000 yearling by Dubawi Legend; major yard runs two and he needs a close look

runners.name: Never Enough
runners.spotlight: By No Nay Never; second foal; dam 5f/5.7f winner (including 2yo/Listed; RPR 108), sister to winners Wild Majesty (French 5f Listed) and Mr Cool (6f), half-sister to useful US 5f-7f winner Big Handsome; newcomer to note.
runners.comment: By No Nay Never; second foal; dam 5f/5.7f winner (including 2yo/Listed; RPR 108)

runners.name: Hemustbeanangel
runners.spotlight: 40,000gns yearling by Dark Angel; third foal; brother to US 5.5f/7f turf winner Chardy Party (including Grade 3; RPR 103), half-brother to 6f 2yo winner Sunny Smile (79); dam 6f 2yo winner (including Listed; 103); early days this term for the yard's 2yos but one was beaten a nose three weeks ago on second outing.
runners.comment: 40,000gns yearling by Dark Angel; early days this term for the yard's 2yos
runners.owner: James Dellaway And Jon & Kay Collins

runners.name: Hey Dude
runners.spotlight: 40,000gns yearling by Showcasing; first foal; dam 1m winner (RPR 65), half-sister to triple 5f Group 1 winner Bradsell, out of 7f 2yo Listed winner; his trainer's first 2yo runner this term and he hasn't had a 2yo winner on turf in recent seasons.
runners.comment: 40,000gns yearling by Showcasing; dam winning half-sister to 5f Group 1 winner Bradsell

runners.name: Il Capo
runners.spotlight: 115,000gns yearling by Supremacy; half-brother to 5f/6f 2yo winner Kairyu (including Group 3; RPR 109); 40-1, however, for maiden at Sandown (7f, good) and he finished sixth of seven after leading for 5f; modest form but he could have come on since that run and perhaps 6f will prove a better fit.
runners.comment: 6th of 7 at Sandown (7f; made most) but could improve and perhaps 6f will be a better fit

runners.name: Adoramus
runners.spotlight: 12-1 for both starts, when third of seven at Lingfield (6f, AW) but well beaten at Pontefract (6f, good to soft) three weeks later; stiff task.
runners.comment: Third of seven at Lingfield (6f, AW) but well beaten at Pontefract (6f, good to soft)
runners.owner: Mr And Mrs R Everist And Partner

runners.name: Its A Ginger Thing
runners.spotlight: 14,000euros foal, 30,000euros yearling; third foal; Space Blues half-brother to 5f winner Boyne Lady (RPR 74); dam twice-raced (60) half-sister to winners Centennial (1m4f Group 2) and Siren's Song (1m2f Listed), out of half-sister to 1m1f/1m2f Group 1 winner Croco Rouge; stable has some pretty useful 2yos and one was successful here on debut in May.
runners.comment: 30,000euros yearling; stable has some pretty useful 2yos and one won here on debut in May
runners.owner: E Kelly, J Woods, N Gallagher & Partner

runners.name: Igrok
runners.spotlight: 25-1, beaten nearly 10l when seventh of nine in novice at Sandown (5f, good) 17 days ago, never dangerous; major work to do.
runners.comment: 25-1 seventh of nine in novice at Sandown (5f, good) 17 days ago, never dangerous

runners.name: Cutlass
runners.spotlight: 72,000euros yearling by Blackbeard; going down by nearly 9l in C&D maiden (good to firm; 18-1) last Monday was low-level form, but he should have benefited from the experience.
runners.comment: Low-level form in C&D maiden (18-1) last Monday but should have benefited from the run

races.race_name: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Divine Libra
runners.spotlight: Both handicap wins have come at Chester (6f and 7f) and he looked to be coming to the boil over the extended 7f there in May, when having a luckless run; rare to see him in 6f turf handicaps these days, though, and has work to do.
runners.comment: Suited by Chester; luckless there in May; rare to see him in 6f turf handicaps these days

runners.name: Cindy Lou Who
runners.spotlight: Three sprint wins in 2025 on extremes of going, including over C&D; excelled herself by going close in a 5f Listed race at Bath on return but she couldn't take advantage of her old mark back in a handicap next time and an 11lb higher mark contributed to an Epsom defeat (5f) since; still not ruled out back over 6f.
runners.comment: C&D winner; excellent Listed run on return; raised 11lb after; not ruled out back at 6f

runners.name: Invictus Gold
runners.spotlight: Quite lightly raced since going up 11lb for a wide-margin win at Newmarket (6f, good) in May 2025, but a recent second on soft ground at Epsom (6f; cheekpieces refitted) was a useful effort at the weights and showed him to be back on track; should be in contention again.
runners.comment: Quite lightly raced since wide-margin 6f win at Newmarket in May 2025; good 2nd latest

runners.name: Regal Envoy
runners.spotlight: Course specialist; in prime form, having won at Bath before suffering a luckless run on this track, then a convincing winner here (also 5f, good to soft; acts well on good to firm) since; that trip seems to suit best though and he has more to prove off 6lb higher in a better race.
runners.comment: In prime form over 5f here, luckless 2nd then won latest; more to do at 6f and up 6lb

runners.name: Fandom
runners.spotlight: Has run all over the place for different yards and in good form for this one, finishing close up in a big 6f York race before winning at Thirsk (6f, good); had a very stiff task in the Wokingham at Royal Ascot where he did well to hang on to his lead for as long as he did on the wrong side of the track; this easy 6f will suit better; can go well.
runners.comment: In form over 6f for new yard; stiff task latest; this easy 6f should suit; can go well

runners.name: King Of Light
runners.spotlight: Narrow 6f defeat at Chepstow last August but his next two runs at the trip were a failure and kept to shorter since, winning at Haydock (good to soft) in September; two respectable efforts at 5f this year (no aids latest) don't suggest this mark is ripe for plucking back at 6f, though.
runners.comment: Has 6f form but below best latest two attempts and raced at shorter since; bit to prove

runners.name: Coul Angel
runners.spotlight: Five 6f wins in 2025, on AW and turf (good); now 14lb higher than his final run on grass last year and, although he faced difficult tasks in two Newmarket starts this spring, he might have run better in the second of them; needs to raise his turf game.
runners.comment: Five 6f wins in 2025 (turf/AW); high in weights back on turf this spring; needs more

runners.name: El Bodon
runners.spotlight: Improved AW form over 6f last winter, but wasn't on his game when last seen on sand in April and he made it 0-11 on grass when beaten about 5l at Newmarket in May; may have his share of weight on balance of turf form.
runners.comment: Improved AW form over 6f last winter but 0-11 on turf and a bit to prove on balance

runners.name: Bolo Neighs
runners.spotlight: Talented 2yo; soundly beaten on turf reappearance in 2025 and was kept to the AW for the rest of the year; probably helped by racing near the stand rail when making a winning return at Ascot (6f, good) in May, but still made a good impression and an early stumble counted against him at Chester since; should still have some improvement in him; thereabouts.
runners.comment: Talented 2yo; raced mostly on AW in 2025; good winning return at Ascot (6f); excuse since

runners.name: Rydale Frosty
runners.spotlight: Won C&D novice then close up in a Group 3 at Glorious Goodwood (5f) last July before losing some momentum; gelded since and was never nearer than at the line on his Newmarket comeback (6f, good to firm) in May; open to improvement and no surprise to see him involved.
runners.comment: Won C&D novice before close up in Group 3 at Goodwood; encouraging reappearance

runners.name: Baldomero
runners.spotlight: Multiple wins but he's won just once on turf in nearly two years and is suited by good or slower ground; two fair efforts of late (no help from the draw at Chester recently) but there may be other days.
runners.comment: Multiple wins but just one on turf in the last two years; best on good or slower ground

races.race_name: Distro Design Handicap

runners.name: Molly Mac
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden but keeps getting placed, last time in 7f handicap at Yarmouth (soft; acts on good to firm) 19 days ago on her only start for Ilka Gansera-Leveque; unraced over this short a trip but it's an interesting move.
runners.comment: 0-10 but keeps getting placed, last time over 7f; first 5f race is an interesting move

runners.name: Apex Star
runners.spotlight: 0-7 but a drop to 5.7f at Bath (good; unraced on firmer) last time produced a front-running second of 11 off today's mark; admittedly beaten 3l but he was clear at one stage and this first attempt at 5f is interesting.
runners.comment: Drop to 5.7f at Bath last time produced a front-running second of 11 off today's mark

runners.name: Harry Brown
runners.spotlight: Nothing like the force of old but he won at Lingfield in May and has been bang there at Newbury and Brighton (beaten a nose) on both starts since, all over about 5f on good to firm; should go well again.
runners.comment: Won at Lingfield in May and bang there on both starts since, beaten a nose on latest
runners.owner: Peter Crate And Gallagher Bloodstock Ltd

runners.name: Diomed Spirit
runners.spotlight: 6-35 for Stuart Williams but 0-11 on turf and, despite a falling mark, that's the obvious concern on this first start for new connections.
runners.comment: 6-35 for Stuart Williams but 0-11 on turf; first start for new connections

runners.name: Just King High
runners.spotlight: AW maiden winner as 2yo but 0-20 otherwise; went handicapping off a high mark and his 3yo season was a write-off; fortunes have turned somewhat off basement marks this term but he's more persuasive on AW.
runners.comment: Fortunes have turned somewhat off basement marks this term but he's more persuasive on AW

runners.name: Kento
runners.spotlight: Races prominently (effective from the front) and he's having a cracking campaign, including when back to turf for first and second in tight finishes at Brighton on last two outings; back up another 3lb and didn't shine here earlier in career, but he needs plenty of respect now.
runners.comment: Having a fine campaign; back to turf for first and second in tight finishes at Brighton

runners.name: Antiphon
runners.spotlight: His last win was in April 2024, which is probably the bottom line; three wins and five seconds from 12 races at Windsor also catches the eye but he was a soundly beaten last at Leicester on reappearance three weeks ago.
runners.comment: Good record here but last won in April 2024 and came last on reappearance three weeks ago

runners.name: Happier
runners.spotlight: 3-31 for Tim Easterby, including a Catterick win (5f, good) last August; second at Lingfield (6f, AW) on stable debut in December but not so good in her three runs since, so others have more pressing claims; some headgear (not used by current yard) returns.
runners.comment: Lingfield 2nd (6f, AW) on stable debut in December but not so good in her three runs since

runners.name: Jowalla
runners.spotlight: Placed in her first three starts over 6f/7f on turf/AW last summer, but has beaten a total of one rival in her four handicaps (some headgear in all four); this is her first go at 5f and a return to good to firm might help.
runners.comment: Poor in handicaps; this is her first go at 5f and a return to good to firm might help

runners.name: The Feminine Urge
runners.spotlight: Two wins off higher marks last season, including C&D, and promising third at Bath this April on her stable/seasonal debut; however, her form has gone the wrong since and she needs to raise her game substantially.
runners.comment: Third at Bath in April on stable/seasonal debut; however, her form has gone the wrong way

runners.name: South Shore
runners.spotlight: 1-1 at Windsor, thanks to a 6f handicap in 2024, and also scored at Wetherby (5.5f, good to firm) last June; overall strike-rate is 2-29 but usually thereabouts and each-way claims on this first start since leaving Ruth Carr.
runners.comment: 2-29 but usually thereabouts and each-way claims on first start since leaving Ruth Carr

runners.name: Cabeza De Llave
runners.spotlight: C&D winner; no win anywhere since October 2024 but he's been placed this year on both his visits to Windsor (5f, good to firm); drawn wider this time but he's considered each-way.
runners.comment: Placed this year on both his visits to Windsor (5f, good to firm); considered each-way

runners.name: Neptune Legend
runners.spotlight: Third run back with Tony Carroll when taking advantage of his reduced mark at Bath (5.7f) last month and he did it in good style; followed up under a 4lb penalty at Brighton (6f, again good to firm) one week later, leading in the final strides, so this 5f could be an issue but he's on a roll again.
runners.comment: This shorter trip could be an issue but he's on a roll again with Bath and Brighton wins

races.race_name: Download The Fitzdares App Now Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: A Taste Of Glory
runners.spotlight: Won a Brighton maiden and 1m2f AW handicap at Lingfield; subsided rapidly to be last of six in the Lingfield Derby Trial (11.6f, good to firm; 33-1) and no show at Epsom (1m4f, soft; 100-1) in the Derby itself, but he returns to handicaps here on what looks a pretty good mark.
runners.comment: 100-1 in the Derby last time; returns to handicaps here on what looks a pretty good mark

runners.name: Twisting Physics
runners.spotlight: Won two of his first four starts before missing the second half of last season; made an eye-catching reappearance from well off the pace this April and went mighty close when he reverted to front-running over that same Newbury 1m2f (good) on latest outing; tongue tied first time in the latter; 5lb rise sees claimer Mason Paetel recruited; leading player.
runners.comment: Two big runs at 1m2f this term; up 5lb, so claimer Mason Paetel recruited; leading player

runners.name: Seagolazo
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner who is 0-13 in handicaps; has a reduced mark and competitive this term, including over C&D, but others are preferred for win purposes.
runners.comment: 0-13 in handicaps; has a reduced mark and competitive this term but others are preferred
runners.owner: Two Plus Three Two Plus Four

runners.name: Bella's Path
runners.spotlight: Looked good in her 2yo win (1m, AW) for Paul Attwater and came from last to first when the 18-1 outsider of six on seasonal, stable and handicap debut at Nottingham (1m2f, good) nine days ago, asserting in good style despite carrying her head a bit high; up 9lb but probably has more to give.
runners.comment: Asserted in good style when 18-1 for her seasonal, stable and handicap debut (1m2f, good)

runners.name: Londoner
runners.spotlight: 3-40 overall; usually thereabouts on turf/AW, including this year, but latest start was an exception and he needs to bounce back from that Ripon run.
runners.comment: 3-40 overall; usually thereabouts on turf/AW but latest start was an exception

runners.name: Moonjid
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with a breakthrough win at Nottingham (1m) and back on the scoresheet by a short head when making all at Yarmouth (1m2f, good to firm) 11 days ago; back up 3lb (5lb claimer is recruited) and should be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Made all for short-head Yarmouth win (1m2f, good to firm) 11 days ago; claimer recruited

runners.name: Explode
runners.spotlight: Pretty useful in light Flat career for James Ferguson last term, before hurdling for new yard didn't really work out; last seen in January and had wind surgery soon after; tongue-tie returns and he has cheekpieces first time, probably needing to better last year's form.
runners.comment: Hurdling for new yard didn't really work out; had wind surgery since last seen in January

runners.name: Greek Order
runners.spotlight: Has some back-class but now 0-10 for this yard and the form he showed in initial outings last summer has not been repeated; edging down the weights but the jockey here on penultimate start reported that he hung badly; others are more convincing.
runners.comment: 0-10 for this yard; edging down the weights but others are now more convincing
runners.owner: S Mizon, H McAlpine & Winner Racing Ltd

races.race_name: Colenko Fast Track Handicap

runners.name: Galactic Glow
runners.spotlight: Easily best known for exploits at Bath, where he's been runner-up three times this season; 0-10 here, admittedly twice runner-up and also third of 15 in May (1m2f) but he may have to settle for another place return.
runners.comment: Best known at Bath (three 2nds this season) but has been placed here

runners.name: Aim For The Bull
runners.spotlight: Pretty consistent since the cheekpieces returned in November and the three wins in that period include 11-runner race at Brighton (1m, good to firm) last time; back up another 2lb and has to be considered.
runners.comment: Going well in cheekpieces; won at Brighton (1m, good to firm) latest; back up another 2lb

runners.name: Love You More
runners.spotlight: 0-12; closest finish when second at Nottingham (1m, good) in April and perhaps 1m2f stretched her when she returned to turf for latest outing; player judged on best efforts.
runners.comment: 0-12; perhaps 1m2f stretched her on latest outing; player judged on best efforts

runners.name: Lady Of The Isles
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden who was fourth in three 1m/1m2f events last year and is edging down the weights; not so good with blinkers enlisted this term and a hood (tried once, she ran well) now joins them.
runners.comment: Nine-race maiden; fourth in three 1m/1m2f events last year and is edging down the weights

runners.name: Raqraaq
runners.spotlight: Both wins on Lingfield AW but runner-up twice from six starts here; having run so poorly back on turf last time is the big stumbling block.
runners.comment: 2nd twice from six runs here; tailing off back on turf last time is big stumbling block

runners.name: Celtic Spirit
runners.spotlight: Lingfield (7.6f, good) last time saw him surge to prominence in his second handicap, coming second of 13 at 33-1; back up 2lb but very lightly raced and could have more to offer in the first-time headgear.
runners.comment: 33-1 second at Lingfield (7.6f, good) last time in his second handicap; first headgear

runners.name: African Spirit
runners.spotlight: Started his career well but it's been poor from April 2024 onwards; has had only two races for current yard and now sports tongue-tie and cheekpieces, both for the first time; 30lb lower than his opening mark.
runners.comment: Started his career well but it's been poor from April 2024 onwards; two races for yard

runners.name: Showmedemoney
runners.spotlight: Again no sign of the tongue-tie he wore for most wins; two runs this season were his first on turf since June 2024 and he needs to return to February's AW levels.
runners.comment: Again no sign of the tongue-tie and he needs to return to February's AW levels

runners.name: Lynda's Dream
runners.spotlight: Huge prices for all four starts and no worthwhile form; latest was her handicap/seasonal debut over 11.4f here.
runners.comment: Huge prices for all four starts and no worthwhile form; latest was handicap/seasonal debut

runners.name: Havana Club
runners.spotlight: Got behind after a slow start at Bath (1m, firm) on penultimate start but still got to within a head of the winner; hampered there when the hood was absent next time; 2-26 overall but one to consider.
runners.comment: Beaten a head on penultimate start; hampered when the hood was absent next time

runners.name: Liberty Bird
runners.spotlight: Both wins came in 2024 when trained by Charlie Johnston; has lost her form and is now with her third trainer since; pulled up here (bled from the nose) on penultimate start; first headgear today.
runners.comment: Has lost her form; bled from the nose on penultimate start; first headgear today

runners.name: Rock Master
runners.spotlight: Several places last year for Sean Woods but none in six starts for this yard and he's now 0-18; off since February and goes beyond 7f for first time.
runners.comment: No places for this yard and now 0-18; off since February and goes beyond 7f for first time

runners.name: Coiled
runners.spotlight: Modest sprint maiden as 2yo but 2025 did not go at all well; absent for a year; changed hands and left Andrew Martin; new yard had a winner on Friday.
runners.comment: 2025 did not go well; absent for a year; left Andrew Martin; new yard had a win on Friday

races.race_name: Find Us At fitzdares.com Handicap

runners.name: Echo Of Faith
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner (7.5f, good) for Jessica Harrington last August but he's finished down the field in three subsequent runs including on handicap/stable debut over C&D (good to firm; 33-1) two weeks ago; still lightly raced but he needs to raise his game.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 3yo but he was well held over C&D on recent handicap/stable debut

runners.name: Trafalger
runners.spotlight: 0-7 but has claims on his best form for Eve Johnson Houghton including a close third in Bath handicap (5.7f, good to firm) last September; returns after 259 days off but he needs watching in market on stable debut; hood reapplied.
runners.comment: 0-7 but he needs checking in market on return for new yard

runners.name: Yachtsman
runners.spotlight: Took his form up a notch when gaining a breakthrough victory in a C&D handicap (good) last month; finished fourth of five at Catterick last time but that was a tactical race and he was beaten less than 2l; this should set up better for his closing style and he's on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Won over C&D on his penultimate run and has claims if he gets a good pace and some luck

runners.name: Lequinto
runners.spotlight: Seven of his 13 wins have come at Windsor and latest was over 5f (good) last month; finished well to snatch third at Bath (5.7f) and Sandown (5f) in his last two runs and he's strongly respected back up in trip.
runners.comment: Seven-time course winner who has form figures of 223133 since April; respected

runners.name: Monsieur Patat
runners.spotlight: Has record of 3-6 over C&D and he's finished placed in his two runs this season including a close second at Newbury (6f, good to firm) last week; on same mark here and he's an interesting contender back at this track.
runners.comment: Record of 3-6 over C&D and he was a close second at Newbury last week; key player

runners.name: Robert Anstruther
runners.spotlight: Reappeared with a promising fourth on handicap debut at Lingfield (7f, AW) in April but he's taken two backward steps since; still lightly raced but he needs a transformation on this return to sprinting.
runners.comment: Seven-race maiden and he needs a transformation on this drop back to 6f

runners.name: Carbine Harvester
runners.spotlight: Four-time AW winner but he's 0-7 on turf and has been well held back in this sphere in three runs after wind surgery; mark continues to fall but he needs to significantly raise his game back up in trip.
runners.comment: Four-time AW winner but he's 0-7 on turf and has been well held in last three runs

runners.name: Amazonian Dream
