runners.spotlight: His last success was in December 2024 but he's on a dangerous mark and wasn't beaten far in a Class 3 over C&D (good to firm) two weeks ago; has possibilities on this big drop back in grade and three of his eight wins have come at this track; visor is removed and cheekpieces return.
runners.comment: Triple course winner who wasn't beaten far in a Class 3 over C&D latest; possibilities

runners.name: Akabusi
runners.spotlight: Returned to form with a win at Newmarket (6f, good) last month but he was back in the doldrums with a 10l defeat over C&D last time; only 2lb higher than for his last success but he comes with risks attached.
runners.comment: Won at Newmarket last month but he was back in the doldrums over C&D last time

runners.name: Mumayaz
runners.spotlight: Won comfortably after travelling strongly at Leicester (6f, good to firm) last month but he's not hit the same level in two subsequent runs; ran respectably back at Leicester last time but he needs to find more to get back on the scoresheet.
runners.comment: Won at Leicester last month but he's not hit the same level in two subsequent runs

runners.name: Northcliff
runners.spotlight: Turned things around when scoring back on turf at Chepstow (7f, good to firm) for Thomas Faulkner last month; remains feasibly treated on that form and he made a solid start for new yard when fourth of 16 at Newmarket (6f, good) ten days ago; in the mix.
runners.comment: Won at Chepstow last month before a fair fourth on stable debut at Newmarket; in the mix

races.race_name: Unibet/EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race) (Div I)

runners.name: Abrahamsen
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f seller at Leicester (good to firm; 11-8 favourite) on his debut five weeks ago despite doing plenty wrong; this will demand more of him but he should be more streetwise with the run behind him.
runners.comment: Won a Leicester seller (7f; 11-8 favourite) on debut despite greenness; this demands more

runners.name: Extended Order
runners.spotlight: Couldn't go overboard about the form of his win at Brighton (6f, good to firm) last month; he did it well enough and today's extra distance should suit but he's likely to be vulnerable under a penalty all the same.
runners.comment: Won well at Brighton (6f, good to firm) last month; 7f should suit; penalty demands more

runners.name: Duke Of Burgundy
runners.spotlight: 9-2, made some late headway into third of nine on debut at Leicester (7f, good) 16 days ago; improvement essential but also quite likely; one to consider on this switch to AW.
runners.comment: Late headway into third on recent Leicester debut; open to improvement; contender

runners.name: Super Alpha
runners.spotlight: Showed some promise in his first two starts (5f, soft and good), after which he was gelded; not so good when favourite at Ripon 11 days ago (6f, good); hopes rest on the extra furlong, plus AW, sparking something extra.
runners.comment: Not progressing and hopes appear to rest on the new combination of 7f and AW sparking more

runners.name: Angel Of Heaven
runners.spotlight: 11th foal; half-sister to seven winners including Firmament (7f/1m including 2yo/AW; RPR 115), Seven Heavens (5f-7f including 2yo/Hong Kong; 107), You're Hired (7f-11.4f including AW; 103) and Romanticizing (1m; 96); dam 7f-1m1f winner (including Group 3; 115); stable have had a couple of 2yos win first time out this year and she's a major player.
runners.comment: Half-sister to 7 winners, from fine family; yard have had 2 winning 2yo newcomers in 2026

runners.name: Sanbona Warrior
runners.spotlight: 37,000euros yearling; second foal; dam 7f AW winner (RPR 70), half-sister to winners Breathtaking Look (6f/7f Group 3) and Super Lover (Spanish 8.5f-10.5f); stable had a winning 2yo newcomer recently (well backed); check betting.
runners.comment: 37,000euros yearling; dam a 7f AW winner; yard had a well-backed winning newcomer recently
runners.owner: J Sarkar, D Sarkar, O Sarkar & D Sarkar

runners.name: Big Bad Storm
runners.spotlight: Attracted support at big prices on debut at Nottingham (6f, good) four weeks ago but finished tailed off; reportedly unsuited by the undulations there but connections have wasted no time deploying headgear.
runners.comment: Nibbled in market prior to Nottingham debut but finished tailed off; now tried in headgear

runners.name: The Monkey King
runners.spotlight: 13th foal; half-brother to six winners including Fox Legacy (1m1f/1m2f; RPR 118), Ceiling Kitty (5f 2yo including Group 2; 104) and Possible Man (1m-1m2f including 2yo/AW; 102); dam placed at 6f-1m (including 2yo/AW; 88), half-sister to Japanese 1m Grade 1 winner Jeweler; already gelded; stable's 2yos are ticking along nicely and he's an interesting contender.
runners.comment: Well-connected newcomer from a stable whose 2yos are going nicely; one to take seriously

runners.name: Baarberini
runners.spotlight: 20,000gns yearling;  fourth foal; half-brother to French 10.5f winner Galimatia; dam French 9.7f winner, sister to US 1m2f winner Galawi, half-sister to 1m3f/1m4f winner Galikeo, out of 1m4f Group 1 winner; looks the part on paper but yard's 2yos yet to hit the target in 2026 (0-7; all unplaced).
runners.comment: 20,000gns half-brother to 10.5f winner in France; yard's 2yos 0-7 (all unplaced) in 2026

runners.name: Lucky Larry
runners.spotlight: 40-1 and always behind in a 5.7f maiden at Bath (good) 24 days ago; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: 40-1 and always behind in a 5.7f maiden at Bath (good) 24 days ago; hard to fancy

runners.name: Golden Oasis
runners.spotlight: 25-1 and little short-term promise when eighth in a Bath maiden (5.7f, good) 24 days ago.
runners.comment: 25-1 and little short-term promise when eighth in a Bath maiden (5.7f, good) 24 days ago

races.race_name: Unibet/EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race) (Div II)

runners.name: Duidin
runners.spotlight: All runs at 6f; improved at the third attempt when winning at Wolverhampton 13 days ago, keeping on well; 7f should suit and he has been found an uncompetitive race in which to try to defy a penalty.
runners.comment: Ready win at Wolverhampton (6f) 13 days ago; major player despite 7lb penalty

runners.name: Singheandaprayer
runners.spotlight: First foal of a 6f 2yo/7f AW winner; stable's juveniles 0-17 this year; likely best watched.
runners.comment: First foal of a 6f 2yo/7f AW winner; stable's 2yos 0-17 this year; best watched

runners.name: Arlecchino's Moon
runners.spotlight: 25,000gns foal, 27,000gns yearling; third foal; half-brother to 7f AW/1m2f turf winner Titainium (RPR 77); dam unraced half-sister to winners Delius (Australian 1m1f/1m2f Group 1) and Nkosikazi (1m2f Group 3); likely best watched.
runners.comment: 27,000gns half-brother to 7f-1m2f winner Titanium (RPR 77); likely best watched

runners.name: Quest For Glory
runners.spotlight: 25,000gns foal; fourth foal; brother to 1m4f AW winner Krona (RPR 80) out of a 1m2f winner (69); likely one for more time and distance.
runners.comment: 25,000gns foal; middle-distance pedigree and he's probably one for later on

runners.name: Lightenings Legacy
runners.spotlight: Eighth foal; half-brother to winners Bullet Point (1m including AW; RPR 111), Pastiche (7f including AW 2yo; 96) and El Salvaje (1m2f AW; 77); dam 5f 2yo/7f winner (including Listed; 102); apprentice ridden and stable not known for 2yo success but he does have a useful pedigree.
runners.comment: Useful pedigree but yard not known for 2yo success and he's apprentice ridden on debut

runners.name: Sparkling Jem
runners.spotlight: Big prices and poor form in two 6f runs this summer; no appeal.
runners.comment: Big prices and poor form in two 6f runs this summer; no appeal

runners.name: Havana Court
runners.spotlight: Makes some appeal on paper and he was 11-2 for his debut at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) 13 days ago; caught a bit wide there and ran with more encouragement than the bare figures might suggest; possible improver.
runners.comment: Ended up well held on Wolverhampton (6f) debut but there was some promise; can do better

runners.name: Half A Hoof
runners.spotlight: Poor form in two sprint runs this summer; new trip/surface not enough to tempt.
runners.comment: Poor form in two sprint runs this summer; new trip/surface not enough to tempt

runners.name: Taseem
runners.spotlight: Big prices and yet to beat a rival in two runs over sprint trips (gelded before latest).
runners.comment: Big prices and yet to beat a rival in two runs over sprint trips (gelded before latest)

runners.name: Rasananda
runners.spotlight: 16-1 and no real threat in a maiden at Leicester (7f, good) 16 days ago; needs to leave that well behind but she is bred to do so at some point.
runners.comment: No real threat in a 7f maiden at Leicester 16 days ago; bred to do better at some point

races.race_name: Unibet More Extra Place Races Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Trio
runners.spotlight: Four respectable efforts in handicaps following her maiden win at Leicester (1m, good to soft) last October; ran well when third here (1m) last month and she now drops into 0-70 company; wide draw not ideal for one who likes to race handily.
runners.comment: Drops in grade and arrives in fair form but a lower draw would have been preferable
runners.owner: M H Dixon & J L Rowsell

runners.name: Hamaleel
runners.spotlight: Front-runner; made all over 1m here in January and has run to a similar level in four runs since, including when blinkers replaced cheekpieces 25 days ago; creeping up the weights and there are other pace influences to deal with today.
runners.comment: Creeping up weights but this course winner arrives in good form; should give her running

runners.name: Emery Down
runners.spotlight: C&D winner last May when trained by John Butler; dropped a long way in the weights since handicapping and her last two runs haven't been too bad; beaten favourite latest but 1m on soft ground looked to stretch her.
runners.comment: C&D winner; still seeking a first handicap win but latest effort suggested it was close

runners.name: Box Clever
runners.spotlight: 7f turf win as a 2yo; promising seasonal return when second of ten at Nottingham (8.3f, good) in April but she took a backward step at Newbury 18 day ago; now tried in cheekpieces for the first time.
runners.comment: 7f turf win as 2yo; promising seasonal return but backward step latest; now tries headgear

runners.name: Searchingtheblues
runners.spotlight: Sprung a 50-1 surprise over C&D on debut (July 2024) and followed up at Wolverhampton that month; 0-11 in handicaps though and her best run this year came in a Class 6; others look stronger.
runners.comment: C&D winner; dropping down the weights but best run this year came in a Class 6

runners.name: Show Me Gold
runners.spotlight: Runner-up at Wolverhampton and over C&D in her two starts this winter; not so good on slow turf three weeks ago but she moves into handicaps with potential.
runners.comment: Runner-up in two AW events this winter, including C&D; below par on turf latest; unexposed

runners.name: Dream Illusion
runners.spotlight: In good form on AW early in the year and she ran her best race on turf when second of ten, beaten a neck, at Wetherby (7f, good to firm) 25 days ago; drawn wide but likely to be dropped in and a strong pace would enhance her claims.
runners.comment: Had a good year on AW and ran well on turf last time; this should be run to suit

runners.name: Daisy Roots
runners.spotlight: Six-time winner, including twice on Polytrack at Lingfield, and her first two runs of the campaign were encouraging; below that level on turf last time but she is the type to bounce back.
runners.comment: Flopped on turf last time but earlier Polytrack runs in 2026 had been promising; chance

runners.name: Mehmas Engine
runners.spotlight: Failed to progress from her debut run (5f, AW) in three further starts for James Tate this spring; sold for 3,400gns last month; up in trip and hooded for this stable debut.
runners.comment: Didn't progress in four runs for James Tate; new trip/headgear on stable debut

runners.name: Ironist
runners.spotlight: Her win came over 6f on slow turf but she has run well over C&D in the past and this return to AW could be beneficial; others appeal more for win purposes though.
runners.comment: Has run well over C&D in the past; this year's turf efforts need bettering though

runners.name: Piazza
runners.spotlight: No improvement for either 7f or handicap company when fourth of five at Sandown (soft ground) when last seen ten months ago but perhaps underfoot conditions weren't to her liking; dropped 4lb and still has potential; Jack Callan's claim is a bonus; usual hood is discarded but she looks interesting.
runners.comment: Unexposed 3yo; should be fine at 7f and she's interesting off a reduced mark after a break

runners.name: Brave Byreflection
runners.spotlight: 6f win here as a 2yo; her only handicap win came in a three-runner affair at Chepstow (7f, good to firm) last summer; she hasn't found her best form in three runs since leaving Alice Haynes and cheekpieces are now reached for.
runners.comment: Course winner (6f); yet to shine for current stable and now tries cheekpieces

runners.name: Tronido
runners.spotlight: C&D winner; career-best RPR when making all up the stands' rail at Lingfield (7f, good; Class 6) last month; creditable fourth at Goodwood since; could face competition for the lead this time.
runners.comment: Prime track position when winning a Class 6 at Lingfield in May; solid Class 4 run latest

runners.name: Something Coming
runners.spotlight: Promise on AW, including C&D, as a 2yo; only midfield on handicap debut when last seen in December; could do better this year but she will need to.
runners.comment: Promise on AW, including C&D, as 2yo; off 193 days; career best needed to shed maiden tag

races.race_name: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling/EBF "Confined" Maiden Stakes (No More Than One Run) (GBB)

runners.name: Tumishi
runners.spotlight: 180,000gns breeze-up 2yo; shaped well when fourth (Primal sixth) at Nottingham on debut 26 days ago, leaving the impression he would have learned from the experience; while the three to beat him have all been beaten since, two were at Royal Ascot and the other was in a Listed race; interesting.
runners.comment: Shaped well when 4th at Nottingham 26 days ago; open to improvement; interesting

runners.name: Primal
runners.spotlight: Sent off a bigger price than Tumishi on Nottingham debut and finished just over 1l behind him in sixth but that may have been a different story had he seen any daylight when trying to launch a challenge; there again shouldn't be much between the pair this time.
runners.comment: Met trouble when sixth (Tumishi fourth) at Nottingham four weeks ago; capable of better

runners.name: Vesper Honey
runners.spotlight: Slowly away and always in rear on last month's Goodwood debut (6f, good; 25-1); not easily recommended on the back of that.
runners.comment: Blew the start and finished well beaten on last month's Goodwood debut (6f, good; 25-1)

runners.name: Adeel
runners.spotlight: £42,500 yearling, £70,000 breeze-up 2yo; third foal; brother to 6f winner Tawasol (RPR 97); dam 6f winner (including 2yo/AW; 78), half-sister to 6f Group 3 winner Tabdeed, out of maiden half-sister to Derby runner-up Walk In The Park; enough paper appeal to warrant a market check.
runners.comment: £70,000 breeze-up 2yo; brother to 6f winner Tawasol (RPR 97); worth a market check

runners.name: Firehorse
runners.spotlight: 85,000euros foal, 95,000gns yearling; first foal of an unraced half-sister to a couple of useful winners; stable's 2yo winner this year won at the second attempt.
runners.comment: 95,000gns yearling; some appeal on paper but likely to come on for this debut effort

runners.name: Fully Stocked
runners.spotlight: 16,000euros foal, 30,000euros yearling, £42,000 breeze-up 2yo; sixth foal; half-brother to winners Hala Blue (6f AW 2yo; RPR 77) and Royal Status (Italian 1m-11.5f); dam Group-placed 6f 2yo winner (93), half-sister to US stakes-placed 5f 2yo winner; stable still seeking a first 2yo win of the year.
runners.comment: £42,000 breeze-up 2yo; half-brother to 2 winners; yard still seeking first 2yo win of 2026

runners.name: Cruse On Bye
runners.spotlight: £10,000 yearling, resold 2,400gns yearling; second foal; dam a maiden half-sister to a winner in Japan; would be a surprise winner on debut, with trainer 0-54 with 2yos in the last five seasons.
runners.comment: Only minor appeal on paper and trainer is 0-54 with 2yos in the last five seasons

runners.name: Sovereigns High
runners.spotlight: 7,500euros yearling, £50,000 breeze-up 2yo; fourth foal; half-brother to Italian 1m winner Witty Indian; dam unplaced at 7f-1m3f (RPR 58), half-sister to 14.5f Group 2 winner Silk Sari, out of unraced half-sister to 1m 2yo Group 1 winner Ibn Khaldun; should stay further in time but still worth a market check.
runners.comment: £50,000 breeze-up 2yo; half-brother to a 1m winner in Italy; betting to guide

runners.name: Kigali
runners.spotlight: 20-1 for his debut at Salisbury (6f, good) two weeks ago, weakening out of it in the closing stages; open to improvement and should last longer today.
runners.comment: Promising start to his career at Salisbury two weeks ago and he should last longer today
runners.owner: Mr And Mrs B V Sangster

runners.name: Stoney Wood
runners.spotlight: Easy to back and never threatened on last week's debut at Windsor (6f, good to firm); of interest only if attracting support.
runners.comment: Never in the hunt on last week's Windsor debut; of interest only if backed

runners.name: Ongombo
runners.spotlight: Dropped away to finish eighth of ten over C&D in April (8-1); gelded since; open to improvement but he is drawn widest and may prove vulnerable.
runners.comment: Gelded since finishing down the field over C&D in April; can do better but drawn widest

races.race_name: Try Unibet's New Smartview Racecards Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Sovereign View
runners.spotlight: Ended his 2yo campaign with a ready win in a small-field nursery at Newcastle (1m); better form when second of seven, beaten a short head, at Southwell in April; flopped on fast turf last month but he could resume his progress back on AW.
runners.comment: Improved with each of his AW starts; latest turf 6th not the run to judge him on; chance

runners.name: Revelance
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with a modest effort at Newmarket but had been making steady progress beforehand; with just seven runs to her name it is not unreasonable to expect further progress as a 4yo; one to consider.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 with a whimper but progressing well beforehand; can do even better as a 4yo

runners.name: Saytarr
runners.spotlight: Hugely progressive for his new stable at the start of the year, winning handicaps at Southwell (7f/1m) before completing his hat-trick at Newcastle (1m); beaten on turf twice since but he is 0-10 in that sphere as opposed to 5-8 on AW; drawn widest but still of some interest.
runners.comment: 3-3 on AW for new yard this year; drawn widest but remains of some interest

runners.name: I'm Workin On It
runners.spotlight: Four course wins over 7f; went close at that trip last month and a subsequent turf defeat isn't the run to judge him on; has had only one attempt at 1m and he appeared to stay; career best needed to win, though.
runners.comment: 4 course wins (7f); latest run excusable but career best needed to win; seemed to stay 1m

runners.name: Mr Baloo
runners.spotlight: Back to winning ways off this mark at Lingfield (1m, AW; cheekpieces first time) in April; he hasn't built on that and others may have his measure.
runners.comment: Won off this mark at Lingfield (1m, AW) in April but he hasn't built on that

runners.name: Chalk Mountain
runners.spotlight: Seven AW wins, the latest on his seasonal return last month when getting the better of I'm Working On It (7f); midfield on turf latest; returning to AW is in his favour but all his wins have come at 7f (0-10 at 1m+) and he could prove vulnerable to stronger stayers today.
runners.comment: Multiple 7f AW winner but 0-10 at 1m+; perhaps vulnerable to stronger stayers again

runners.name: Arctic Thunder
runners.spotlight: His ability is not in question but he needs everything to fall right and has often flattered to deceive; stepping back up to 1m is worth a go but others look safer.
runners.comment: Talented but often gets going too late; 1m is worth another crack but others appeal more

runners.name: Enemy Agent
runners.spotlight: Beat a good horse when winning over C&D on debut; struggled in two turf runs since but the return to AW could see him tap back into that debut promise; blinkers are tried today after one run in cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Looked good prospect when winning over C&D on debut; struggled on turf since; could revive

runners.name: Showcasing Star
runners.spotlight: Finished second on his first three starts (AW; around 1m); never a threat on handicap/turf debut (7.6f, soft) 17 days ago; back on AW but needs to prove this mark is in range.
runners.comment: Well held on handicap debut (7.6f, soft) 17 days ago; return to AW can help; tough mark

runners.name: Director's Cut
runners.spotlight: 1m AW win for the Gosdens in November; not so good in two turf runs over further in April; sold for 28,000gns in May; new stable quick to drop him in trip and fit headgear.
runners.comment: 1m AW win for the Gosdens last year; new stable quick to return him to 1m and fit headgear

runners.name: Farasi Lane
runners.spotlight: Ended his losing run at Wolverhampton (8.6f) in March and he did well to follow up over 7f one week later; not quite so good since but he has the ability to go well off this mark; headgear tried last time is now quickly dispensed with.
runners.comment: Ended losing run in March (8.6f) and followed up over 7f one week later; each-way shout

runners.name: San Juanito
runners.spotlight: Two AW wins for former stable; latest turf run is not the race to judge him on but, even so, he comes here with plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Better on AW, so latest turf run excusable; still comes here with plenty to prove though

runners.name: Percy's Lad
runners.spotlight: Thrown in on 2024 best but he missed 2025 and has yet to get going this time around; hopes appear to rest on the drop in class having a positive effect.
runners.comment: Missed 2025 and yet to shine this year; drops in class but with a point to prove

runners.name: Dutch Kingdom
runners.spotlight: Seven-time AW winner for Darryll Holland, notably of a 7f handicap at Southwell in March 2025 when 5lb higher; well beaten in three runs for Jim Boyle and, although the last two were on turf, enough to prove even back on AW.
runners.comment: Return to AW is in his favour but he still has enough to prove

races.race_name: Try Unibet's New Improved Acca Boosts Handicap (London Middle Distance Qualifier) (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Fox Avatar
runners.spotlight: Dual Polytrack winner (1m2f/1m4f) last summer and was returning from 279 days off when sixth of 13 in a 0-90 contest here (1m4f) last month; that run may well have brought him on and he remains unexposed for a 5yo after just nine starts; high on list.
runners.comment: 3-9; should be all the better for last month's return; high on list back down in grade

runners.name: Golden Horse
runners.spotlight: Sent off 15-8 favourite for his handicap debut/reappearance at Yarmouth (1m2f, good to firm) in April but appeared to hang his chance away on the fast ground; brother to an AW winner and half-brother to another; a better performance this time would be no great surprise.
runners.comment: Disappointing on handicap debut, but bred to handle the AW and likely to perform better

runners.name: Crystal Mariner
runners.spotlight: Not fired in two starts on turf since returning last month but he won twice over C&D last autumn and is just 1lb higher than for his latest success; worth a second look.
runners.comment: Won twice over C&D last autumn; 1lb higher than for latest success; worth a second look

runners.name: Assail
runners.spotlight: 3lb lower than when winning here (1m4f) in September 2024 and ran well to finish fourth of ten in a 0-105 contest over C&D (first-time blinkers, now removed) in January but a five-month break and high draw do pose questions.
runners.comment: 3lb below last winnng mark but a five-month absence and high draw pose questions

runners.name: Charmaine
runners.spotlight: Consistent mare whose three wins on the AW include two here (1m/1m4f); still on a fair mark and should again run her race.
runners.comment: Three wins on the AW include two here; still on a fair mark and should again run her race

runners.name: Metallo
runners.spotlight: 0-11 on the AW and 1-18 overall; down another 2lb and cheekpieces are given a go this time, but he still has stamina to prove over this far.
runners.comment: 0-11 on the AW and 1-18 overall; still has stamina to prove over this far; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Naval Command
runners.spotlight: Five-race maiden who has finished out of the frame in two turf handicaps since returning last month, the latest when fitted with this headgear combination; another 2lb drop not enough to tempt.
runners.comment: 0-5; out of the frame in two turf handicaps since returning; 2lb drop not enough to tempt

runners.name: The Green Mile
runners.spotlight: Has endured a couple of lengthy absences in his seven-race career but showed the ability remains when successful in a first-time hood (now removed) at Chepstow (1m2f, good to soft) last month; half-brother to a couple of AW winners and shortlisted; stable also runs Mathematician.
runners.comment: Won at Chepstow last month; half-brother to a couple of AW winners; shortlisted

runners.name: Mathematician
runners.spotlight: Won impressively over C&D for Roger Varian in September 2023 but not seen since; returns for his new yard off an 8lb higher mark and best watched after such a mammoth absence, unless the market suggests otherwise.
runners.comment: Won impressively over C&D for Roger Varian in September 2023, but not seen since

runners.name: Gloryous
runners.spotlight: Twice made all on turf (1m2f, good) last July but has been off for eight months and was well held in one previous go on the AW (Tapeta), while she remains 8lb above her last winning mark; market useful; stable also runs Crystal Mariner.
runners.comment: Off for eight months and remains 8lb above her last winning mark; market useful

runners.name: Tipsy Tiger
runners.spotlight: Placed a couple of times on turf last autumn but no win since September 2024 and still 2lb higher; has run with credit here, but likely he will need this after 252 days off.
runners.comment: Winless since September 2024 and still 2lb higher; likely to need this after 252 days off

runners.name: Diderot
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner on Polytrack (1m/1m2f), but yet to click since returning for this yard in January, refusing to race on one occasion; has dropped a long way in the weights, but risky.
runners.comment: Yet to click since returning for this yard; dropped a long way in the weights, but risky

races.race_name: Bet £20 Get £20 With Unibet Handicap

runners.name: Balgowan
runners.spotlight: Simply bumped into one on a rare turf start six weeks ago and returns to Polytrack off the same mark; cheekpieces go on for the first time in almost a year and his claims are obvious.
runners.comment: Bumped into one on a rare turf start six weeks ago; cheekpieces return; claims are obvious

runners.name: Break Point
runners.spotlight: Couple of fair efforts for this (his fifth) yard, latterly over hurdles a few weeks ago; better treated on the Flat but was twice beaten at short prices during the winter and has had any number of chances.
runners.comment: Couple of fair efforts for this (his fifth) yard; better treated on Flat; one to consider

runners.name: Golden Samba
runners.spotlight: Just behind Balgowan from the front at Lingfield (turf) six weeks ago before giving herself no chance of seeing it out last time (testing 2m1f) thanks to pulling too hard; may well get her own way if that's the plan and should fare better.
runners.comment: Latest run best ignored; may well get her own way if that's the plan; should fare better

runners.name: Sneaky Blinder
runners.spotlight: Two course wins to his name from four starts; none of his six victories have come off a mark quite this high and he's lost his way since going in at Wolverhampton in March; needs a revival.
runners.comment: 2-4 round here but lost his way since going in at Wolverhampton in March; needs a revival

runners.name: Apache Eagle
runners.spotlight: Handful of fair efforts in the spring but he's a longstanding maiden who's lost his way of late; again hung left at Hamilton last Thursday (ended up on the stands' rail) and running at another right-handed course would be a worry.
runners.comment: Longstanding maiden who's lost his way of late, again hanging badly left last week

runners.name: Nelson Gate
runners.spotlight: Beaten a nose here last August (1m4f) off 2lb higher; disappointing run over hurdles five weeks ago, when a remote last of three, will need forgiving, but he has claims otherwise in a weak race going this far for the first time; back to blinkers from cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Claims in a weak race going this far for the first time; back to blinkers from cheekpieces

runners.name: Our Papa Smurf
runners.spotlight: Tended to be all or nothing under both codes when last seen; entitled to need this after more than a year off (first Flat run in going on two years) and Nelson Gate looks his yard's best chance.
runners.comment: Tended to be all or nothing under both codes when last seen; entitled to need this
